stem: A 24-meter-tall vertical wall runs along one side of a rectangular courtyard. When the sun is directly opposite the wall with an angle of elevation of 45 degrees, the wall casts a rectangular shadow across the courtyard. The courtyard is 50 meters long (parallel to the wall) and 35 meters wide (perpendicular to the wall). What is the area of the shadow cast on the courtyard, in square meters?
explanation: The shadow length (perpendicular to the wall) is found using tan(45) = wall height / shadow length. Since tan(45) = 1, the shadow length equals the wall height: 24 meters. Since 24 < 35, the shadow fits within the courtyard width (the 35-meter width is extra information). The shadow is a rectangle with dimensions 24 meters (shadow length) by 50 meters (courtyard length parallel to the wall). Area = 24 * 50 = 1,200 square meters.

distractorRationale.A: Multiplied the shadow length by the courtyard width instead of the courtyard length: 24 * 35 = 840
distractorRationale.B: Applied the triangle area formula (1/2 * base * height) instead of the rectangle area formula: (1/2) * 24 * 50 = 600
distractorRationale.D: Computed the total area of the courtyard floor instead of the shadow area, ignoring the wall height calculation entirely: 35 * 50 = 1,750

imageDescription: A 3D perspective view showing a vertical wall (24 m tall) along one side of a rectangular courtyard. The sun's rays come from the right at a 45-degree angle. A shaded rectangular region on the courtyard floor represents the shadow. The courtyard is labeled 50 m along the wall and 35 m perpendicular to the wall. The shadow extends 24 m from the base of the wall.
